backlog.title: 5 frames of coyote time
backlog.canon_risk: Physics/speedrun contract — this is the sanctioned kind of touch: purely additive input forgiveness, zero existing movement constants changed (GRAV, JUMPV, speeds untouched). Still contract-adjacent, so it gets a CHANGELOG version note and a fullrun-bot pass, per the same rule that covered the existing 6-frame jump buffer.
backlog.implementation_sketch: In the physics block ~line 1288: `p.coyote = p.onGround ? 5 : Math.max(0, p.coyote-1);` and widen the jump gate at ~1293 to `(p.jbuf>0 && (p.onGround || p.coyote>0))`, zeroing p.coyote on jump so it can't double-fire. Completes the forgiveness pair the game half-shipped (buffer without coyote), verified eaten-input pit deaths by both physics-literate personas.

backlog.title: Juice + motion-comfort pass: boss-hit weight, audible damage confirm, reduced-motion gate, kill the 15Hz flicker
backlog.canon_risk: Three rules: (1) hitstopT/shakeT are set in update() only — line 4053's own comment enforces it ('sim owns all state'); (2) ruling 2.2 already blesses hitstop-as-juice; (3) owner steady-visuals pref makes replacing the frame%4 hard strobe a directive, not a nice-to-have.
backlog.implementation_sketch: (a) In the boss-damage branch (~1605): hitstopT=8; shakeT=4; and hitstopT=20 on b.hp<=0 — the pattern already exists at line 1198. (b) Layer beep(110,.2,'square',.13,-70) on damaging stomps only, so armed hits and harmless bounces are audibly different. (c) At boot: `const RMOTION = matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;` and when true skip the draw-only translate at 2613 and zero uiShakeX/Y at their draw sites (documented draw-only, so no sim impact). (d) For everyone, replace the boss i-frame frame%4<2 sprite-skip (line 2096) and rocket-flame strobe with steady cx.globalAlpha=0.5 while hitT>0 — same signal, no photosensitive-band strobe, no flicker.

backlog.title: End-card fold and occlusion pass (name field, rotate banner, timer vs pause chip)
backlog.canon_risk: none — all DOM/coordinate work outside the sim.
backlog.implementation_sketch: Four small fixes in one pass: (1) in showEndUI() hide #rot (GENZ verified it covers the leaderboard name-input row in portrait) and let the existing fit() restore it on resume; (2) when innerHeight<620, reorder the template-string DOM so the name/POST VALUATION row renders above the button rows (FOUNDER's macOS hidden-scrollbar case); (3) append '· or press R' to the '= VALUATION' line so the keyboard affordance sits above the fold (HN); (4) move the canvas timer box from W-62 to ~W-130 so the fixed #pauseChip stops occluding the mm:ss readout (A11Y, screenshot-verified).

backlog.title: PostHog privacy gate (or first-party counter) before the HN share
backlog.canon_risk: One-file rule is about the GAME's required files — analytics is already an optional third-party tag gated off file:// for probe hermeticity, so gating it further is strictly canon-positive. The full first-party swap adds an api/ endpoint, which is the sanctioned place for server bits.
backlog.implementation_sketch: Minimum (2 tokens): extend the gate above line 37 with `&& navigator.doNotTrack !== '1' && !navigator.globalPrivacyControl`. Better, when there's an hour: delete the us-assets.i.posthog.com loader and POST a 5-line count event to a new api/ping.js alongside the existing three functions, keeping the page first-party-only — which is also the pre-repo-public hygiene the CLAUDE.md TODO list already gestures at. Single persona, but it's the persona who writes the top HN comment, and the finding is verified at line 37.

backlog.title: Share-surface truth pack: unicorn gate, pedigree on the board, STANFURD footnote, per-run og:description
backlog.canon_risk: Rule 1 (satire in mechanics): showing 'x100' next to a pedigree-inflated board entry IS the joke — where you dropped out of beats what you built, now legible on the leaderboard. Rule 3: STANFURD stays an archetype-safe rivalry gag; the footnote just signals intent. Celebs/billboard grep-gates unaffected (no names touch share text).
backlog.implementation_sketch: (a) Gate the badge headline on winEmoji's existing $1B bar: `v>=1e6 ? 'CERTIFIED UNICORN' : 'CERTIFIED HORSE (for now)'` (~line 3585) — a funnier flex ladder that makes $1B worth grinding. (b) Add pedIdx to the leaderboard POST body, store the column, render a dim 'x100' suffix in lbRowsHtml; scope the 'cosmetic. like most pivots.' disclaimer to the C/H/V chips only. (c) One 6px footnote in drawTitle: '*stanfurd is not a typo. ask berkeley.' (d) In api/r, template og:description from the query params it already receives ('ASSOCIATE raised $10K, died with a $5K valuation (down round).') instead of the generic tagline. (e) Self-gloss the T2D3 flair: 'T2D3 (sub-3:00 exit)'.

backlog.title: PIVOT roulette: make the retry button's joke mechanically true
backlog.canon_risk: Rule 1 is the REASON to do this — the FOUNDER persona correctly cited the game's own canon against 'PIVOT (RETRY)' being label-only. Rule 3: pivot names stay invented archetypes ('a CRM for dogs', 'same product, but agentic', 'we do defense now') — no real companies. Draw-only strings; sim untouched.
backlog.implementation_sketch: A const PIVOTS=[...] array and a counter incremented in the retry path (update-side state, read by draw). Draw 'THE COMPANY: '+PIVOTS[i%len] under the zone banner after each respawn, and pipe the current string into the obituary's company line so death N's newspaper mourns pivot N. Single-persona, but it came from the designated butt of the joke and it upgrades the funniest button in the game from label to mechanic — humor is the product.

do_not_do.idea: Asymmetric fall gravity (p.vy += vy>0 ? GRAV*1.4-1.7 : GRAV) — STAFF-ENG and RETRO-GAMER both want it
do_not_do.why: It changes existing movement numbers, which is exactly what the speedrun contract forbids as a casual fix: fall speed determines horizontal pit clearance, the code itself admits one pit was frame-perfect, and RETRO's own suggestion concedes JUMPV would need retuning to preserve the apex. That's a full physics release — retune JUMPV, revalidate every pit, rerun both fullrun bots against a same-day baseline worktree, versioned CHANGELOG note — not a playtest patch. The finding is real; ship coyote time (additive, rank 10) now and schedule gravity as its own deliberate version if ever.

do_not_do.idea: Horizontal acceleration/skid curve replacing the instant vx=±1.7 (RETRO-GAMER)
do_not_do.why: Same contract problem in disguise: an approach-lerp changes effective acceleration and turnaround distances even if top speed is 'identical', which shifts pit-edge and enemy-dodge timing that speedruns and the bots depend on. The cursor-snappy movement is also part of the shipped feel identity at this point. Cosmetic turn-dust particles are fine; the physics change is not.

do_not_do.idea: Held-jump variable stomp bounce (p.vy = JUMP() ? JUMPV : -4.5 at line 1504) (RETRO-GAMER)
do_not_do.why: Changes a movement constant (bounce height -5 → -6.6 when held) — contract-touching — and interacts with the carefully-shipped pogo re-arm rule that keeps held-jump chains from cheesing bosses. High rebalance surface for a feel nicety two personas didn't even agree on.

do_not_do.idea: lastSafeX respawn system for pit deaths (NONTECH-MARKETER, GAME-DESIGNER)
do_not_do.why: It's a new difficulty mechanic, and canon says difficulty is tuned via existing levers, never new mechanics. It also creates fresh edge cases (last safe ground = a crumbling ledge one tile before the pit → respawn-loop death). Adding checkpoints[] entries (backlog rank 3) delivers the same mercy through the sanctioned lever with zero new code paths.

do_not_do.idea: Boss post-stomp invulnerability window (FOUNDER) and 2s fresh-spawn i-frames (GENZ-MOBILE, HN-GRAYBEARD variant)
do_not_do.why: Both are new mechanics wearing a tuning costume. If THE PLATFORM dies too fast to blind mashing, raise its HP pip count — the existing lever. If SOMA's opening strips runway too fast, move the first gremlin spawn — the existing lever (backlog rank 3). Spawn i-frames additionally hand speedrunners 2s of god-mode off the line and teach new players nothing about the stomp verb.

do_not_do.idea: setInterval/setTimeout-driven music sequencer (several personas sketched setTimeout-chained loops)
do_not_do.why: Wall-clock timing is canon-banned for a reason the music loop would violate continuously: it would keep playing through pause, blur-autopause, and hitstop, and drift against the fixed-timestep sim. The loop must derive its step index from playMs in update() and schedule notes on AC.currentTime with lookahead (backlog rank 7 does exactly this). One-shot stings at state transitions should likewise use AC-time offsets rather than setTimeout chains.
